narrative_beat: Close-up lateral editor vitoriano anonimo, 50 anos, oculos, pena de tinta sobre pergaminho

style_reference.textual_description: dark cinematic chiaroscuro, Caravaggio-inspired, foreground in rich warm tones (crimson, amber, gold) with background in desaturated black-and-white, film grain, candlelight, floating orange embers

subject.primary: an anonymous Victorian-era male newspaper editor, approximately 50 years old, with round spectacles and severe expression, holding a dip pen hovering over a parchment, viewed from a 3/4 angle with strong chiaroscuro lighting
subject.focal_point: the dip pen poised above the parchment
subject.secondary: Inkwell and scattered papers on desk.

composition.foreground: editor in rich warm tones — ochre vest, amber lamplight on spectacles
composition.background: desaturated black-and-white Victorian editorial office with typesetters

lighting.key_light: warm oil lamp from left illuminating the face and parchment

style.post_processing: halation, crushed blacks, amber highlights, anamorphic lens flares
style.texture_detail: 35mm film grain, dust particles, floating orange embers

mood.emotion: solemn declaration, the weight of the written word

symbolism.primary: the editor as oracle — writing what he knows will happen
symbolism.secondary: dip pen as weapon of revelation

negative_as_positive: clean frame without visible text or watermarks
photorealistic documentary cinematography, not illustrated or cartoon
analog film photography aesthetic, no digital art flatness
muted desaturated background palette, warm saturated foreground only
anonymous figure without recognizable facial features
silhouette or contre-jour lighting hiding identity

multimodal_inputs.instruction: Transfer color palette, lighting, grain, atmosphere only. Do not copy composition.
